reviews.review_text:
Absolutely recommend staying here.
Staff were incredibly friendly and knowledgeable.
Gorgeous RedPlain 1  unpowered bush camp was assigned to us by Zoe and it did not disappoint.
The skytrek was the best 4WD experience we’ve had. The helpful notes developed by Station family and staff were exemplary.
The diversity of the landscape and the stewardship of ancient lands is commendable.
The nearby Stoke hill lookout was tremendous.
It really was a joyous and immersive Station stay. Thanks for having us.
We will be back.
Kaye, Andy and Liam.

Absolutely beautiful place. We stayed 2 nights in one of the immaculately maintained unpowered, very private Corella sites, which offered the most amazing bush camp experience. The incredibly magical 4WD Skytrek took us just over 7 hours, with the last 15kms being the trickiest and challenging. The staff are very friendly and interactive, very knowledgeable of the land and support conservation with ongoing programs. One of our most favourite places. We will be back.

Completed Skytrek 4wd trail on motorbike. Great track with stunning scenery. Every corner you turn different things to look at. Trail on bike is manageable & also challenging at times but we'll worth it. Staff very friendly & informative.

We originally planned to camp 3 nights, but extended to 6 nights after staying at the Corella campsites. Very private site with no one around, and clean showers  a 1km ish drive away.

Skytrek drive was also fantastic. Highly recommend.

A very big thank you to the team at Skytrek Willow Springs Station.
We stayed at the Homestead for two nights and completed the 4 wheel drive circuit. It was an amazing stay.
The Homestead is wonderful. Great home bristling with history, super comfy beds, great showers, amazing fire pit, and the most stunning location. The team there are so friendly, helpful and awesome.
The 4wd loop was an amazing way to enjoy the stunning Flinders Ranges up close and surrounded by their beauty. It was an awesome day with stunning scenery, views and thrilling 4wding. It really was the highlight of our travels. We’ll definitely come back the next time we visit the Flinders 😀

Visited in April 2026. A beautiful and secluded location. Stayed in the Jillaroos Hut -- clean, comfortable and well equipped for self-catering, at good value. An excellent alternative to the pricey WP Resort. The road in and out could be challenging after heavy rain if you don't have a 4WD.

This place deserves 5 star for a great outback (but not too outback) experiences. Michelle and her staff were just helpful and make us feel home. Amazingly being at the heart of Flinders Ranges. Will definitely come back!
What an amazing 4wd day out. Staff are so friendly. Arrive 8am and started this 80km track into what is some of the most breathtaking scenery in Australia.

What a wonderful camping experience!
Bush camping sites are allocated and are all well graded, large and reasonably flat areas (all with picnic tables, fire pit and a loo). The various site that are well spaced from each other and giving you that remote feeling.
We couldn’t do the 4WD due to the recent rains but embarked on of their 12 km well described walking trail (linking 2 shorter trails).
Just stunning country site and if can only do one trail, go for the yakka lookout.

We had a couple of nights in the Stockman’s Cottage, enjoying the peace and solitude of this remote station. It’s located just 3km off the paved highway on a good all weather road, but you really feel like you are out in a remote area. Bring all your own supplies as the nearest restaurant and shop are 30 minutes away at Wilpena Pound. Our 2-bedroom cottage was amazing with large picture windows overlooking a big deck and the surrounding forested hills – really a magical outlook! The interior was bright, spacious, clean, and comfortable. The kitchen was fully functional, suitable for preparing just about anything. A large woodstove adorned the center of the room, good for those cool winter nights. There is no cell phone signal and the Wifi signal doesn’t extend all the way to the cottage, however, you can receive a decent Wifi signal out on the deck which was sufficient for our needs. Overall we had an amazing stay and would definitely recommend it to other travelers.

Willow Springs Station is well located to be your base camp when touring the area. We stayed at a bush camp site. It was well serviced with a private toilet, large table with bench seats, a fire pit and BBQ plate to cook on. Campers can utilise the showers, laundry and camp kitchen located near the reception building. It was a 1klm drive from our camp to the amenities. There are plenty of day trip drives to do and the surrounding landscapes are amazing. Lots of the drives could be done in a standard car. Their are plenty of 4WD tracks for those interested. The Skytrek drive at Willow Springs is a stunning full day's entertainment. The last 15klms has some serious low range 4WD driving
Stayed at a beautiful bush camp. The Skytrek drive is brilliant. Fairly tame for the 1st 65k or so, however the last 15ks is serious low range rock crawling up and down steep hills.
Nice people, great track!

Great location in the Flinders Ranges. The cabin was clean and comfortable for a night. The only issue is that there's no Wi-Fi at the cabins, it is only at the central camp kitchen (which may be a plus if you want to disconnect). The office staff were incredibly friendly and helpful with things to see in the area and even what time to go watch sunset at the lookout nearby.

Reviewing a hiking trail: rugged country yet easy walking, well signposted, great views, information leaflet describing the main points of interest available and best of all, I had it all to myself. Great experience, thank you for the opportunity!

We were offered our own very private camping spot for our caravan with a clean, well maintained porta loo onsite. The gravel area was raked beautifully clean, no dust or mud. We simply rode our push bikes to the shower block when needed. For people who peace and privacy this is a great option!

What a fantastic 4x4 track. We have done this twice now and love the written commentary and landscape. There are certainly some more challenging areas but certainly doable for most 4x4s. You will need a bit of ground clearance when you get to the engage 4x4 section. There’s a few larger rocks to negotiate. We had some mechanical issues and the helping hand we received was next level. Thanks team for helping us out and being dog friendly

If you're camping in Flinders Ranges, this place is a must. Very spacious private camp grounds available with private toilet, small general store at reception, great views. I didn't have time to do their 4x4 tracks, but that's on the books for next time. Much cleaner than I was expecting and much better than the earlier location I stayed at. I wish someone had put me onto this place earlier. Very cheap too, but don't tell them!

Our family was very happy with our stay at this campsite.
The facilities were exceptionally clean, well-maintained, and beautifully organised.
Everything ran smoothly, and it was clear that a lot of care goes into keeping the grounds tidy and welcoming. We had a fantastic time and would happily return. - highly recommended!

Willow Springs|Michelle Reynolds greeted us with a huge smile (even tho it was nearing the end of her busy day) & very helpfully suggested things to do & see on & near Willow Springs.|All amenities are immaculately clean, with a well equipped kitchen/community tv & wifi area available to everyone, even available for use by self sufficient campers.|- [ ] The perfect location for exploring the Flinders & Wilpena areas, & easy gateway to many places of interest.|- [ ] Perfect for families, so much nature to explore…& skytrek gives adults & kids an Adrenalin hit that will never be forgotten!|- [ ] Our campsite Corella 2 was beautiful, secluded & with our own campfire place & cook plate. Creek was dry but great for fossicking, kids & adults alike!|- [ ] Highlight was sunset drink at Stokes Hill lookout 10min from Willow Springs.. sunset over ranges 360 view is speccy.|- [ ] All round, Willow Springs is a must to see & stay.|- [ ] Congratulations to the Reynolds family for the years of hard work, passion & dedication in bringing the Flinders to life for so many.|- [ ] ||Lina & Pat
Beautiful location lots of walks and night sky telescope viewing option - and dog friendly too- yeah.
Our 3rd trip to Skytrek. As always a great day with great info supplied.
Great place, and service! Just be careful near camp 3 in the overflow area as there's an uncovered well under a windmill. Super dangerous as we have young children running around
We didnt stay but would in future and the 80km sky trek track was a great 4wd trip
Beautiful property and the Skytrek drive covers the diversity of the landscape out there.
Did the sky trek 4wd and loved it
Great place

An excellent camping spot in the Flinders Ranges on a working station. Lots of secluded sites, good clean facilities and very helpful friendly staff. It also has a 8 hour 4x4 track with 50 POI, called Sky Trek.

Skytrek is long, very very rough and not worth it unless you are a hard-core 4WD enthusiast. The first 68km are around the property. Skip that and just do the last 14km for the actual Skytrek section.

We stopped at Willow Springs on a spur of the moment decision and we were not disappointed. Our relatively secluded camp site had its own portaloo, fire pit and plenty of room only 900m from showers & laundry. We did the SkyTrek drive which was well worth the whole day in the car. Amazing ever changing scenery with great views & interesting geology. Friendly service & super clean amenities. Highly recommend
Great place to base yourself for your flinders visit.
Wonderful place, happy helpful staff,  and Skytreck was well worth the trip.

Willow Springs and Skytrek have much to offer but our review here is for accomodation in the Overseers hut. and hut it is..... small, old and basic. we found many places this could be improved, particulary the wierd bedhead which gave us both big lumps on the scone! Their is an abundance of WIFI in the camp but not available for this hut, you must go walkabout to find some. the shower is so pathetic.
On a thumbs up note.... loved the fire pit and included wood, the TV and air cond and elec blankets. Great place to explore and it could be a bike riders destination if the owners put thier mind to working out some trails over the various farm tracks.

We booked a private site for our large caravan and we got a fantastic spot close to the homestead where all the amenities are. We even had our own portaloo at our site which meant we didn't have to use or empty our van loo.
We absolutely loved our stay at Willow Springs.
We just went there to do the Skytrek which was amazing.
Some good 4x4 action to be had on the last 20ks (or so) of the Skytrek.
The staff/owners were all very friendly and super helpful.
Highly recommend a stay here.
Thank you Willow Springs for sharing your property with us.

We had a great spacious campsite close to amenities. The Skytrek Trail did not disappoint; we had a fantastic time. The trail features marked distances and numbered points of interest, and the journey took us 8 hours.
We enjoyed the stunning scenery and wildlife, including ochre cliffs, steep ascents, rugged climbs, and the ranges changing colour throughout the day. It was an epic 4WD adventure we’ll never forget!
Great camping, staff were super friendly and helpful. The Skytrek was outstanding. Views the whole way are amazing.

Wanting to stay around the Flinders Ranges for a few more days, we happened upon Willow Springs station.|What a fantastic place. From initial booking,  to arrival and welcomed by Michelle we absolutely loved this place.|We were given camp site 3 which was private, with picnic table, fire pit and portaloo. Such a neat and clean area with plenty of room for us. We noticed camp sites 1,2,4 and 5 were all large sites perfect for bigger groups or families. All sites are spaced far enough apart so as not to be on top of each other.|We were blessed with perfect weather enabling plenty of walks around the station soaking up the magnificent scenery, smells and sounds. Abundant bird and wildlife was another bonus. Amenities were clean and spacious, only a short drive from camp.|We did not undertake the Skytrek, however, all the reviews from fellow campers were ‘it was not to be missed’.|From our camp we were central to a lot of the local Flinders Ranges attractions, Blinman, Stokes hill lookout, Hucks lookout, Wilpena pound, gorge drives and so much more. The Reynolds family run a perfect place to do as much or as little as you like. |We will definitely be recommending to family and friends and be returning ourselves in the future.
Great location to enjoy the Gorges and walks.

Stayed in Governess Cabin for 2 nights , was really good . Grateful to the host who were helpful and knowledgeable.

I will definitely recommend this to everyone who I know wants to visit flinders Ranges.

❤️
Perfect stop for a Flinders advetures. Very friendly and also helped in sorting out further accommodation in the Ranges.

Had 3 nights camping at "Joe's" site.  Recent rains made the drive in quite muddy but not a problem for 4wd off road vans.  Great site with firepit, picnic table and even a porta-loo.  The base was elevated and well packed so rain and mud not an issue.  You'll need everything with you as no water etc.  byo firewood.
Skytrek was closed due to the rain so we took other drives in NP which were open, and challenging drive through creeks etc.
It's a beautiful setting, remote yet still easy access back to Wilpena Pound should you need it.
The central camp kitchen/showers/laundry etc was only 1km away if required.
We'll come back again for Skytrek.

Staff detailed information about everything this area has to offer. We spend 4 days here in Joe's Camp on the property which was its own private camp spot loved it. BBQ plate over the fire pit, sit back and listen to the quiet. Look at the stars and a must is Skytrek 4x4 track on this property 80 kilometres of amazing landscapes and views forever don't miss it. Big shout out to Michelle thankyou and all of the very professional staff.

Amazing stay for three days, two nights. The four wheel drive track is great. The first 80% is pretty much just a bush track with some challenging climbs here and there, but once you're through the  locked gate, I'd recommend some good tyres and a rear locker minimum. If there's been rain, it'll be a slippery, steep and slow climb. First time four wheel drivers will love the challenge, the experienced will have no problem. Make sure you're stocked up with booze, the station has yet to get a license, but are looking at it! Make sure you  enjoy the sunset at the nearby lookout. We loved our stay. Shower blocks and camp kitchen were great!

What an awesome station to stay at. Great people, amenities and campsites.
Skytrek track was fantastic and the last 12kms was a challenge especially after some of the rain in the Flinders Ranges.
Definitely worth it and we will be back!

We spent 5 nights in the Stockman’s Lodge in June. Michelle, Carmel and George were amazing hosts during our stay, always happy to offer advice on things to do and must go places. The lodge was super comfortable, super clean and very well appointed, the double-sided fire was a highlight, firewood topped everyday like clockwork! The walks around the working property were fantastic and offered an insight into the workings of a remote sheep station. Incredible feeling of privacy even though they were busy every night whilst we were there. Close by to Blinman, Wilpena, Brachina and the Glass Gorge drive. We’ll be back again as 5 days was most definitely not enough for us!|Highly recommend

Stunning location close to everything! We spent 5 nights in the Stockman’s Lodge in June. Michelle, Carmel and George were amazing hosts during our stay, always happy to offer advice on things to do and must go places. The lodge was super comfortable, super clean and very well appointed, the double-sided fire was a highlight, firewood topped everyday like clockwork! The walks around the working property were fantastic and offered an insight into the workings of a remote sheep station. Incredible feeling of privacy even though they were busy every night whilst we were there. Close by to Blinman, Wilpena, Brachina and the Glass Gorge drive. We’ll be back again as 5 days was most definitely not enough for us!
Highly recommend
Great Staff, Fun day out on Skytrek

Communication with Michelle at Willow Springs started with a very prompt exchange of emails and phone calls.|All queries were answered quickly and in detail.||The location is spectacular. While it has been quite a while since there has been any rain, and it is still a working sheep station, there are still some ‘roos and the scenery is fantastic.|Another bonus of the centrally located property is that access to many of the most popular spots is easily very manageable. These include Brachina and Bunyaroo Gorges, Wilpena, and Sacred Canyon.|There are also delightful walks on the property itself.||The accommodation we had, the Stockman’s Lodge, was very well equipped including an electric blanket 😁😁.  In our eight nights, we lacked for nothing. In addition, it was sufficiently far enough from all other accommodation that we felt we were the only ones there. In fact, the entire property was quiet as a mouse.||On top of all of that, the staff were friendly, helpful, and most knowledgeable on a range of things including with whom to book tours, the conditions of the roads with up-to-date information, help with geology, identifying animals and plants, and even help with our car warning system.||It is made clear that no food is sold on the property, and that all food must be brought in. To compensate for that, the refrigerator and freezer were more than ample for our eight nights.||Especially considering the cost of transportation for everything, Michelle and her family have managed to keep the rates at a level that provides very good value.

Communication with Michelle at Willow Springs started with a very prompt exchange of emails and phone calls.
All queries were answered quickly and in detail.

The location is spectacular. While it has been quite a while since there has been any rain, and it is still a working sheep station, there are still some ‘roos and the scenery is fantastic.
Another bonus of the centrally located property is that access to many of the most popular spots is easily very manageable. These include Brachina and Bunyaroo Gorges, Wilpena, and Sacred Canyon.
There are also delightful walks on the property itself.

The accommodation we had, the Stockman’s Lodge, was very well equipped including an electric blanket 😁😁.  In our eight nights, we lacked for nothing. In addition, it was sufficiently far enough from all other accommodation that we felt we were the only ones there. In fact, the entire property was quiet as a mouse.

On top of all of that, the staff were friendly, helpful, and most knowledgeable on a range of things including with whom to book tours, the conditions of the roads with up-to-date information, help with geology, identifying animals and plants, and even help with our car warning system.

It is made clear that no food is sold on the property, and that all food must be brought in. To compensate for that, the refrigerator and freezer were more than ample for our eight nights.

Especially considering the cost of transportation for everything, Michelle and her family have managed to keep the rates at a level that provides very good value.

Fantastic venue for large groups. Sleeps up to 32. Combination of double bedroom, bunk beds, 2 single beds. Internet free but a 5 minute walk to access if required. Plenty of hiking and 4WD opportunities. 40 mins back to Wilpena Pound. Scenery is spectacular.  Take plenty of extra blankets or warm clothing if visiting in Winter.
Skytrek 4x4 track is a must do!
Great campsites, awesome facilities and great staff
Great 4x4 track if you’re up to it! Beautiful views and amazing wildlife.
Amazing property, very well organised, great people. Weather was terrible this weekend but we thoroughly enjoyed our stay and will definitely be back!
Brilliant place on the heart of the Flinders Ranges, just a stones throw from Wiloena Pound.

We stayed in the Caravan. Clean, tidy and friendly. Toilets ,kitchen and showers spotless. Your own fire pit. Or share with others, lots of sightseeing around the area. Hosts cannot do enough for you,  outstanding.

Stayed 3 nights in the camp number one horse yard.
Great spot to camp next to the dry creek
Beautiful spot plenty of bird life
Did the 4 wheel drive track of 80 klms
Fantastic especially the last 20 klm
Highly recommend this station stay
Stayed in Overseers Cottage. Perfect for 2. Great option for doing Flinders Ranges. Happy didnt stay at one of the resorts (Wilpena or Rawnsley), and this was much less busy and felt authentic
One of the best 4x4 tracks i have been on

We stayed in the Shearer’s cottage.|So comfy and everything we needed to be comfortable.|We liked the area too - felt it more central to Blinman, Parachilna and Wilpena, more so than other stays.|We didn’t have a 4x4 but that was not a worry in our RAV4.|Loved the bird life and the beautiful area in general.|Would highly recommend.
Such a hidden gem. This was our 4th visit and the place never fails to deliver.
The place recommended for watching the sunset on that day was really great and very friendly. Thank you very much

Absolutely loved our stay at Willow Springs Station! The campsite was immaculate, peaceful, and beautifully maintained — one of the cleanest we’ve ever been to. The customer service was top-notch — warm, friendly, and genuinely helpful from start to finish.

We also did the Skytrek 4WD off-road experience, and it was incredible! The track was well-marked with stunning views and challenging sections that made for an exciting adventure. Highly recommend it for anyone who enjoys a bit of thrill in the outback!

This place has it all — nature, adventure, and hospitality. Can’t wait to return!
Had a fantastic time at Willow Springs Station and doing ‘Sky Trek’ was an experience we will never forget! 🤩
Did the Skytrek for the third time and loved every minute including the rain and panoramic views from the hill tops.

WOW highly recommend Willow springs as a station stay. We have a private campsite with portable toilet. This is the cleanup ever. We completed the Skytrek 4WD today which took about 6 1/2 hours to do 80ls. The views and wildlife was fantastic.

Willow Springs was great spot to chill for a few days, great trek walks & great camp spots on the property.
We had a hideaway area which was 💯 %
Freindly owners & very informative 👍
Highly recommend Willow Springs 👌

Skytrek is an 80km long self drive/guide track through some of South Australia’s most spectacular arid and hard country side. Designed to give participants and their vehicles a reasonably challenging 4x4 trek through spectacular gorges and dry river beds lined with beautiful Red Gums to sparsely vegetated plains and stunning ancient scorched hills, all part of the Flinders Ranges. You start and finish at the WillowSprings Station homestead and receive detailed trek notes and guide that includes a lot of  interesting information and history to get you immersed in your journey and help pass the hours.  They recommend you allow 8 hours but we took 6 and a half.
You will see many of the iconic animals of inland Australia.  My wife and I were a little hesitant at the price but after completing the entire Skytrek we were happy to have done it and have a newfound respect for our vehicles 4x4 capability.  Roughly the last third of Skytrek is suitable only for 4x4 capable vehicles and the other two thirds would suit most SUV’s

Enjoyed our stay very much. Peace and quiet. 4wd track was excellent and abit gnarly towards the end. I had birds landing on me at breakfast to be hand fed some crumbs. The wife was happy for the WiFi. Thanks to the fire pit I was able to crissen my campoven. Overall a fantastic stay, will be back and will be spreading g the word. Than,s so much willow springs.

Amazing facilities and kept clean!
Staff are incredibly helpful and friendly
Skytrek is 10/10 and is a must do!
Great location to enjoy many trail hiking options

We had a fantastic experience at Skytrek Willow Springs! This is a charming, family-run campsite with great infrastructure. Michelle gave us such a warm welcome and went out of her way to explain the best routes for our journey. Her tips were incredibly helpful and made our trip planning so much easier. We couldn’t have asked for better hospitality! Highly recommended for anyone looking for a well-organized and friendly camping spot.

Great outback camping experience. Fantastic views. We were in the "back paddock" camp sites, as we were a large group, so only a drop toilet. But access to all facilities at the main camping ground. Limited Wi-fi at the camp kitchen. Fantastic showers. Did the skytrek 4wd route. Nothing overly challenging.. Mostly easy, with a small section of medium skill level.

THANKYOU Michelle & family for a FANTASTIC stay at Willow Springs Station!! We thoroughly enjoyed the serenity & privacy of the Grasswren campsite which we shared with 21 dear friends, countless grazing sheep by day & majestic wild horses by night. The 4WD experience on SKYTREK was an absolute highlight of our ten day Flinders Ranges Adventure & couldn’t recommend it highly enough. Technical, informative & exhilarating! Facilities were excellent & the assistance your family provided us was invaluable seeing as we were travelling in such a large group. THANKYOU!
Really wonderful family run accomodation. Loved the stockmans lodge, perfectly fit for purpose! Will definitely be back in the future.
Wonderful location and views.  Great Staff and hospitality.  Skytrek is a must do.

Willow Springs camping and sky trek were the highlight of our flinders range trip.

The unpowered camp sites were each about 50 meters apart and each campsite was large enough for several tents and vehicles - and each had a spotlessly clean portaloo.

The sky trek experience was fantastic! You do need to be an experienced 4 wheel driver with a real 4 wheel drive car to do it. They give you several pages of information about the history and geological features and interesting spots on the trip.

Great camp sites, ours had its own porta loo. Kangaroos in close proximity to the camp site daily.
Next nearest camp site was far enough away that we could not hear them.
We did the skytrek including the harder section, well worth the cost, awesome scenery,  you don't need to be an off-road expert but I would say the harder section is not for a complete novice either.

Brilliant place to stay if you love calm and cozy places!! It's got some azimg lookout views and the service is great too!! George - the caretaker is an amazing person and gave us loads of information about the place which helped us decide where to go upto!
And, gazing at the sky to see million stars is a great highlight to this place.
Highly recommended!!

Skytrek drive was fantastic! Interesting reading along the way with very well signposted points of interest and information within the map / booklet to read up on. Views were amazing and incredible property to explore and enjoy.
Thank you team. Look forward to visiting again.
Love this place, staff are friendly and welcoming and everything is set up well, great facilities

We stayed at campsite 3 on the 8/9/24 and arrived late. We were met with great friendly service despite our late arrival. Facilities are great and staff are informative. A lot of thought went into the campgrounds, for example there was a storm water trench dug to stop water running off the hill and flooding the campsite. A lot of care used with maintaining the campsite, clean grill and bench. Even the ground has been raked! Didn't have time todo the Skytrek so Willow Springs will be our first stop when returning to the Flinders Ranges.
Very friendly and helpful

Had a great Homestead camping stay. Very secluded site with a porta loo and showers available at homestead.  The sky trek self drive tour was awesome. We thought we'd knock it off in a couple of hours but it took 7hrs as described with great information booklet to explain what we were seeing along the way. Highly recommend for anyone who likes an adventure 4wd camping holiday.
Lovely place 😊

Booked 4 nights, but unfortunately had to cut short due to illness.
Before it all went belly up, our breathe was taken away by your beautiful backyard, The Flinders Ranges. You were welcoming, full of clear instructions and recommendations which made our stay so easy to work trips.
Missing out on the Stellar Stargazing due to illness, really was not the best.
We will be back, to finish what we begun. We will openly recommend you to all our friends.
Had a wonderful time at this place, I didn’t want to leave. Lots of places to explore. The Skytrek was amazing x
Interesting spot to spend a couple of days. Convenient location to seeing various things in Northern Flinders

Highly recommended staying at the station. We camped in tents & had access to the camp kitchen which was super clean. Also the toilets & showers were fantastic. Did the Skytrek track & loved it. Well mapped out & spectacular scenery. Staff went well & above. We will be back

The most amazing place for peace, rest and stargazing. We stayed in one of the cabins which was fully equipped with everything we needed. The following day we completed Skytrek which was the highlight of our holiday…the most amazing , stunning, exhilarating and beautiful experience 😍😍

We had an amazing 3 night stay at Willow Springs Skytrek Station.
We did the Skytrek track and it was absolutely gobsmacking brilliant. Well worth the $70.

Stokes Hill Lookout - 😲😲😲 what an awesome view of the sunset. Easy to get to.

It was truly an outback experience staying within the Willow Spring Station

We had a great time doing the 4WD.

We stayed here recently on our travels to Flinders Ranges. We had an excellent time. Campsites are big & private. Amenities well maintained and clean. Our hosts were so helpful, great info on the local area & things to do & see. Highly recommend!

Very lucky to get a spot here, perfect quiet location. Only neighbours are the wildlife. $35 a night, porta loo provided. The fire pit was great with a hot plate over the top. Staff were very friendly and accommodating. The skytrek is a must, breathtaking views and a breathtaking 4WD experience towards the end of the trek.
Very quiet good central location to explore the ranges

We were camping.  There is plenty of room and a good clean cleared area for our group of 5 vehicles for our personal use. My first foray into 4wding and  Skytrek was a great introduction and outstanding scenery leading up to it.
Our favourite station stay. Plenty to do, we loved our large private camp-site,  stunning views, great service. We're heading back for another stay before we head out of the Flinders.

Great location and facilities available!
The staff are so friendly and knowledgeable.
We stayed in a campsite and had a lovely time. The fire pit, toilet and table provided were clean and ready for use.
We also did the 4wd SkyTrek which was an all day adventure - travelling through creek beds, farm yards and along high ridges. All the lookout points, lunch spot and experiences were great!
Will definitely be coming back for another trip.
Great spot to camp for a few days. Paid to do their 4wd track & was well worth it. Great track.
Amazing private camp sites, Skytrek drive was highlight and highly recommended
Beautiful location with friendly staff.  The cottage was lovely and comfortable.  Sky trek itself was a great experience with the accompanying information very helpful.  A wonderful spot.

We drove the skytrek track, and stayed one night in an unpowered site. The 4x4 track had spectacular views, was a good challenge and very well signed with a great accompanying handbook. The track took us around 7 hrs, and was a highlight of our time at the Flinders rangers. The bush camp site was very secluded, lots of space and privacy, with an outdoor table, and dunny. Highly, highly recommend.

My friend and I camped up there years ago - can't remember the year but was bad year for the locusts we were there for a week with 2 horses. Had a fantastic time The owners supplied extra feed for our horses as even though we had some with us we were partly relying on natural grasses, but due to the locusts there was none available

Was happily surprised with how wonderful this place is! Originally booked it as we wanted to travel to the Flinders Ranges and stay over night and it was reasonably priced and close. This farm stay is more than a accommodation, it is a mini get away. The location onsite offers many activities, from 4WD tours, to hiking, to even having an onsite physicists to teach you about the stars. My only wish is that we had more time there, make sure to allow a few days to just explore the grounds. The cabins themselves, as well as the camp kitchen, and toilets were all extremely clean.
Wonderful place to visit to enjoy the Flinders Ranges. Skytrek is a must do for 4 wheel drive enthusiasts.
Awesome views and plenty of interesting information for the track!
Best spot we've ever camped. Secluded, animals everywhere, great amenities only a short drive away, toilet on-site. Serene as it gets. Just the best
Great place to stay with excellent facilities and friendly staff. Skytrek isn't for the faint-hearted on big motorbikes but a real test and great views along the way.
Great location! Hot showers, laundry, great Wifi and a fair price.

Great spot, excellent camp spots, especially for non powered sites
Good central location for exploring The Flinders Ranges.

Nice to finally find a station that gives you a rurap experience ratger than cramming you into a caravan park on their land. Their unpowered sites feel like no one is arround for miles. They even come with their own portaloo which is one of the cleanest toilets youll expereince whilst travelling australia.
Really well set up camping and surprisingly cheap for what it is

We loved this place. It was dry, dusty, kind of harsh, but that is the season. Welcome to this end of the Country. Sky Trek was fantastic. Sky Tour was really enjoyable. The family that runs the place were hospitable. Not in your face but there if you wanted it.

Absolutely perfect spot to base camp if you are visiting the ikara flinders ranges, nestled pretty much in the centre of the national park me and my partner had our own private bush camp that we stayed at for 3 nights in our offroad caravan. Each bush camp even has its own personal flushing porta loo! A short drive down the the station itself and their are beautiful clean einsuites to clean yourself each night and also a camp kitchen. Highly recommend 👌

Amazing place to enjoy the beauty of the Flinders ranges.
The campsites are very well groomed and equipped with enough space between each site to have some privacy.
The capacity is reasonably limited which makes the stay really enjoyable.
The 4WD trek is beautiful, well maintained, with many patches of history to learn from.
We took our time and finished at dusk with a mesmerising moon and active fauna to enjoy. I wish we could camp along the trek.
Great job from the Reynolds family.

Station and staff were amazing. Service from staff and family was warm and welcoming. All staff were very helpful and had great tips. Camping sights were amazing we stayed in Joes. Remote feeling just a short walk to all facilities. Skytrek drive was amazing and little fact sheet supplied by station keeps you informed the whole way.

So beautiful! We stayed at corella 3 & wouldn’t have known anyone else was anywhere.  Great fire pit with swivel bbq plate wildlife wandering past and completely off grid - LOVED it. Staff were so welcoming and friendly A+++
Just spent 5 nights at a fantastic camp site as a base for the Flinders Ranges, great setup and amenities, really enjoyed the stay, also do the Skytrek 4x4 tour, highly recommend this place
Good showers,  with lots of hot water
Great Adventure completing Sky Trek amazing views, great location, great family history.

A great place to consider staying when in the Flinders Ranges. It's around a 15 min drive to Wilpena Pound and very central to most of the scenic drives. The accommodations vary in type, size and age and are good value compared to many accommodations in the area. They are clean and well equipped. If you have a 4x4 they also have their own 80km plus 4x4 track on the property. Even the entry to the property can almost be a 4x4 track at times after rain. I would recommend it as a place to stay. Of course it also has several camping areas, some isolated in the bush and  several van spots.

Best showers ever had at a camp-site
Nice established camping sites
Great fun, challenging sky trek course
Great staff
We loved it!
